title: cerastium fontanum subsp. grandiflorum
intro: Cerastium fontanum subsp. grandiflorum, commonly known as large flowered mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. It is known for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant is also rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to promote overall health and well-being, with reported benefits including reduced stress, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Cerastium fontanum subsp. grandiflorum can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
